proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability is 'shifting' and only 1 day in Bear; if regime reverts to Choppy quickly, cohort will deactivate and any new Bear-gated positions may be left orphaned
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.4pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Regime is Bear (day 1, shifting) so the cohort is now active, but portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/77 (14.3%) while the 85.71% concentration trigger is on Choppy — promotion does not compound the flagged concentration, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-19) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +28.2pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
BULL_PROXY currently only 3/77 (3.9%) of open exposure, leaving headroom for incremental Bear-gated activations under TAKE_SMALL sizing
Regime stability is 'shifting' (day 1 Bear) — if regime reverts to Choppy, cohort auto-deactivates without harm
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 is borderline and remains under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance); a small adverse shift could trigger demotion on next archive comparison
n=17 is modest; one or two additional losses could materially move Wilson lower bound
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +28.2pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +28.2pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear and current regime just shifted to Bear (day 1, shifting); portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/77 (14.3%) while the 85.71% concentration trigger is on Choppy, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound flagged concentration and may diversify. Consistent with prior approval of this exact cohort on 2026-06-19 and adjacent UP_TRI×Bear approvals; no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type = Choppy regime (85.71%) + UP_TRI signal (92.2%) (85.7%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type = Choppy regime (85.71%) + UP_TRI signal (92.2%); recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy AND signal_type=UP_TRI until either Choppy share drops below 80% via natural Day-6 exits OR Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes to Tier M; consider index/futures hedge sized to ~20-30% of long delta to neutralize regime-flip tail
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the dominant UP_TRI thesis across 71 positions with no statistical edge backing the exposure; sector clusters in Bank/FMCG/Pharma would unwind together, amplifying drawdown beyond typical single-name variance.

evidence: Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort tier=Candidate (n=234) — unvalidated edge
Top concentration cells (Bank 15.58%, FMCG 11.69%, Pharma 11.69%) all map to Candidate-tier cohort
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy AND signal_type=UP_TRI until either Choppy share drops below 80% via natural Day-6 exits OR Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes to Tier M; consider index/futures hedge sized to ~20-30% of long delta to neutralize regime-flip tail
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the dominant UP_TRI thesis across 71 positions with no statistical edge backing the exposure; sector clusters in Bank/FMCG/Pharma would unwind together, amplifying drawdown beyond typical single-name variance.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.8571 (Choppy, above 0.80 threshold) with the matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort only at Candidate tier (n=234) — unvalidated edge. UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 71/77 = 92.2% (above 0.85 threshold) with the book overwhelmingly directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 74/77 = 96.1%). No single cell breaches the 0.20 threshold (top cell Bank at 15.58%), but regime + signal-type concentration on unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
